questions.q: Chanakya is also widely known by which other name?
questions.explanation: Chanakya is famously also called Kautilya (and Vishnugupta).

questions.q: Chanakya is traditionally credited with authoring which treatise?
questions.explanation: Chanakya is traditionally credited with the Arthashastra, a manual of statecraft.

questions.q: Which ruler did Chanakya guide to power?
questions.explanation: Chanakya mentored and guided Chandragupta Maurya to the throne.

questions.q: Chanakya helped overthrow which dynasty of Magadha?
questions.explanation: Chanakya engineered the overthrow of the Nanda dynasty.

questions.q: The empire founded with Chanakya's guidance was the:
questions.explanation: Chanakya's efforts led to the founding of the Mauryan Empire.

questions.q: Chanakya is best described as Chandragupta's what?
questions.explanation: Chanakya served as Chandragupta's principal adviser and strategist, the 'kingmaker'.

questions.q: The Arthashastra is primarily a treatise on what subjects?
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra deals with statecraft, economics, diplomacy, and military strategy.

questions.q: Chanakya is popularly remembered with which subtitle in this deck?
questions.explanation: Chanakya is celebrated as 'The Kingmaker' for raising Chandragupta to power.

questions.q: Chanakya lived around which period?
questions.explanation: Chanakya flourished in the late 4th century BCE during the founding of the Mauryan Empire.

questions.q: Chanakya is principally remembered as a scholar and what?
questions.explanation: Chanakya was a scholar and political strategist who shaped Mauryan statecraft.

questions.q: Chanakya is traditionally associated with teaching at which ancient centre of learning?
questions.explanation: Tradition links Chanakya with the renowned centre of learning at Taxila (Takshashila).

questions.q: A third name by which Chanakya is known in tradition is:
questions.explanation: Chanakya is also called Vishnugupta in several traditional sources.

questions.q: Which classical Sanskrit play dramatises Chanakya's political schemes against the Nandas?
questions.explanation: Vishakhadatta's Mudrarakshasa portrays Chanakya outmanoeuvring the Nandas' minister Rakshasa.

questions.q: In the Mudrarakshasa, Chanakya's chief rival, the loyal Nanda minister he wins over, is named:
questions.explanation: The minister Rakshasa is Chanakya's worthy adversary in the Mudrarakshasa, eventually won to the Mauryan side.

questions.q: The Arthashastra famously discusses a fourfold set of policy expedients for dealing with rivals. Which is one of them?
options: Sama, dana, bheda, danda (conciliation, gifts, division, force)
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra outlines sama, dana, bheda and danda as the four means of statecraft.

questions.q: The Arthashastra advocates an elaborate network of which agents for state security?
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra recommends an extensive system of spies and informants to safeguard the state.

questions.q: According to tradition, what insult triggered Chanakya's vow to destroy the Nandas?
options: He was humiliated and expelled from the Nanda court
His ship was seized at sea
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Chanakya was insulted and dismissed from the Nanda court, prompting his vow of revenge.

questions.q: The Arthashastra describes the state through a model of how many essential elements or limbs of sovereignty?
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra's saptanga theory lists seven limbs of the state, including the king, ministers, and army.

questions.q: Collections of pithy moral and practical maxims attributed to Chanakya are commonly called:
questions.explanation: The Chanakya Niti (and Chanakya Nitishastra) are collections of maxims traditionally ascribed to him.

questions.q: Chanakya's home capital, from which he served the Mauryas, was the city of:
questions.explanation: Chanakya advised the Mauryan court from Pataliputra, the imperial capital.

questions.q: Chanakya's statecraft is often compared by modern writers to which European political thinker?
questions.explanation: Chanakya is frequently compared to Machiavelli for his pragmatic, hard-headed political doctrine.

questions.q: The surviving text of the Arthashastra was rediscovered and published in the early twentieth century largely through the work of:
questions.explanation: R. Shamasastry rediscovered a manuscript of the Arthashastra and published it in 1909, with a translation soon after.

questions.q: The Arthashastra is composed in which form and language?
options: Sanskrit prose with verse, in sutra-and-commentary style
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra is a Sanskrit text in terse prose interspersed with verses, organised into books and chapters.

questions.q: The Arthashastra is divided into how many books (adhikaranas)?
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra is traditionally arranged into fifteen books covering different aspects of governance.

questions.q: Modern scholars debate whether the Arthashastra is the work of a single author or a compilation refined over centuries. This debate centres on what?
options: Whether it was carved on Ashokan pillars
Whether it was written in Greek first
Whether 'Kautilya' authored all of it or it was edited later
questions.explanation: Scholars debate whether the text is wholly Kautilya's or a layered compilation finalised by later editors.

questions.q: The Arthashastra prescribes a state office overseeing weights, measures, and prices headed by an official with what kind of role?
options: A high priest of the realm
A superintendent (adhyaksha) of a specific department
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra details many adhyakshas, departmental superintendents, regulating commerce, mints, and more.

questions.q: Which work of Greek-derived information on Mauryan administration broadly corroborates the kind of bureaucracy the Arthashastra describes?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes' Indica, describing Mauryan governance, broadly echoes the administrative detail of the Arthashastra.

questions.q: The traditional account of Chanakya finding and training Chandragupta as a boy is preserved especially in which tradition's texts?
questions.explanation: Buddhist and Jain texts preserve legends of Chanakya discovering and mentoring the young Chandragupta.

questions.q: The Arthashastra's view of foreign policy is often summarised by the concept of the 'circle of states' known as:
questions.explanation: The mandala theory frames neighbours as natural enemies and enemies' enemies as allies in a circle of states.

questions.q: Chanakya's enduring significance lies chiefly in shaping which lasting feature of the Mauryan state?
options: Its system of caves and stupas
Its centralised administration and governing theory
questions.explanation: Chanakya's ideas underpinned the Mauryan Empire's centralised administration and theory of governance.
